results.title: Senior Manager, Geospatial Intelligence – Localized Assortment & Store Clustering at The Kraft Heinz Company - United States Remote
highlights:
Senior Manager, Geospatial Intelligence – Localized Assortment & Store Clustering at The Kraft Heinz Company - United States Remote
...
# Senior Manager, Geospatial Intelligence – Localized Assortment & Store Clustering
...
🕒 June 19
...
🇺🇸 United States – Remote
...
💵 $120.8k - $151k / year
...
⏰ Full Time
...
🗺️ GIS
...
food and beverage
...
### 📋 Description
...
• Develop and maintain store-level clustering strategies based on shopper behavior, trade area composition, demographic shifts, and emerging market trends. • Leverage Placer.ai and other data sources to create micro-assortment recommendations. • Analyze store-level and market-level data to identify current and future localized opportunities that influence category strategy and assortment decisions. • Build event-, weather-, and season-based playbooks that help customer teams and partners respond to changing local demand patterns. • Identify local and regional seasonal opportunities, including multicultural events, temporary population shifts, and other market-specific drivers of demand. • Conduct incursion analysis to evaluate the impact of new store openings near existing locations and recommend actions to mitigate risk or unlock growth. • Synthesize data from the Category Intelligence Platform into clear business insights and recommendations. • Present actionable, data-driven recommendations that support both strategic planning and local execution.
...
### 🎯 Requirements
...
• Bachelor’s degree in GIS, Geography, Analytics, Business, Statistics, Data Science, Economics, Marketing, or a related field; advanced degree preferred. • Significant experience in geospatial analytics, retail analytics, category management, localized assortment, space planning, or related commercial strategy roles. • Experience using Placer.ai or similar mobility/location intelligence platforms. • Strong understanding of trade area analytics, shopper behavior, store clustering, and localized retail strategy. • Demonstrated experience working with integrated datasets that connect geospatial, shopper, and store-level operational data. • Proven ability to convert complex analyses into clear business recommendations that influence customer-facing and internal decisions. • Strong communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to operate as a subject matter expert across multiple audiences.
...
United States – Remote

### Principal Data Scientist at [Kraft Heinz](https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-kraft-heinz-company) (Current)

Lead agentic AI solution development in commercial, marketing and R&D.
...
* Architecture & Platform: Architected and built an enterprise GenAI consumer insight platform using LLM parsing pipelines, Ray-based distributed computation, and Snowflake-powered semantic agents, enabling scalable insight generation across 1,000+ CPG brands. * Commercial Science Agentic AI: Designed and developed agentic LLM workflows that converted Power BI dashboards (revenue, product, ingredient analytics) and Python-based simulation tools into conversational AI applications, reducing analyst query turnaround from hours to minutes. * Multi-Agent Design: Built multi-agent systems (routing, layout-aware RAG, LLM-as-judge, financial analytics agents) enabling structured reasoning and decision support for commercial teams. * Business Impact: Influenced GenAI investment strategy and secured $1M+ annual funding to launch and scale an enterprise GenAI Center of Excellence; drove platform adoption to 100+ active users across business units. Innovation: Filed 2 AI patent applications with USPTO (Query Direction Method, Topic Modeling); recipient of 2025 and 2026 Priority Patent Awards. * Enablement & Leadership: Led AI adoption enablement by training data scientists, business analysts, and technical managers on Snowflake Cortex agents, Microsoft 365 Copilot agents, and Claude-based Skill workflows. Partnered cross-functionally with engineering and business teams to productionize AI workflows.

results.title: Oliver Ganschar
highlights:
VP of Data, AI & Digital Innovation | Leading Enterprise Digital Transformation at Kraft Heinz
...
VP of Data, AI & Digital Innovation at [Kraft Heinz](https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-kraft-heinz-company)
...
With extensive experience in digital transformation and innovation, I serve as VP of Data, AI & Digital Innovation at Kraft Heinz, where I lead enterprise‑scale data, analytics, AI, and digital product capabilities with a clear focus on measurable business value. I am responsible for shaping and executing a comprehensive digital and AI strategy across the end‑to‑end value chain, working closely with Sales, Marketing, R&D, and Operations to embed advanced analytics, AI, and digital products into core processes and decision‑making. The objective is to drive sustainable growth, operational excellence, and consumer value at scale. Leveraging expertise in mathematical modeling, computer vision, and optimization, my teams design and deploy high‑impact solutions that improve productivity, enable smarter decisions, and strengthen execution across the organization. I am deeply committed to fostering a culture of innovation and empowerment ensuring technology, data, and AI translate into real‑world outcomes that position Kraft Heinz as a digitally enabled, data‑driven enterprise.
...
#### VP of Data, AI & Digital Innovation (Current)
...
As VP of Data, AI & Digital Innovation at Kraft Heinz, I lead the creation of enterprise‑scale digital capabilities that turn data and AI into measurable business value. The role focuses on embedding advanced analytics, AI, and digital products into core operations and decision‑making driving growth, efficiency, resilience, and consumer value across the end‑to‑end value chain.
...
As the Head of Digital Product Management and Innovation at Kraft Heinz, I am entrusted with leading the charge in transforming the organization into a fully autonomous digital enterprise. My role encompasses designing, leading, and executing a comprehensive digital strategy that spans all functions of the business, including Sales, Marketing, R&D, Operations, HR, and beyond. By leveraging cutting-edge technologies and driving innovation, I aim to create seamless integration across all business functions, fostering a culture of digital excellence and enabling Kraft Heinz to remain at the forefront of the industry. My focus is on increasing organizational productivity, enhancing digital capabilities, and empowering teams through coaching, mentoring, and promoting new ways of working. Through this role, I am committed to not only driving operational efficiency but also ensuring Kraft Heinz is positioned as a leader in attracting and retaining top talent in the digital age.
...

My love for data can be traced back to age five when I dressed up as Sherlock Holmes for Halloween, determined to solve every mystery in sight. results.title: Gustavo F. Augusto
highlights:
Head of Business Intelligence & Digital Transformation at [Kraft Heinz](https://www.linkedin.com/company/the-kraft-heinz-company)
...
With over 20 years of experience in sales development, market and consumer research, strategic planning and market analysis, my career has been marked by transforming data into valuable insights and business opportunities. My expertise in data science and analytics, combined with my ability to solve analytical problems, has enabled me to lead high-complexity and high-impact projects across different sectors, both in national and international markets. Currently, as Head of Business Intelligence and Digital Transformation at Kraft Heinz, I lead projects that generate analytical insights to enhance team effectiveness in relation to key strategic objectives. I manage and create dashboards with crucial information for various areas of the company, conducting exploratory analyses to identify problems and develop innovative solutions. Previously, had different senior roles such as: Head of Business Intelligence, Analytics & S&OP at Raízen and Regional Market Manager LATAM at Sherwin-Williams. My career also includes positions at companies such as AkzoNobel, LATAM Airlines, Competitiveness Strategy Consulting and Itau-Unibanco, where I consistently stood out in creating and implementing analytical models and strategies that resulted in cost reduction and sales growth. My academic background includes an MBA in Business Administration from EAESP-FGV, a Certificate in Data Analytics for Managers from the University of Michigan, and a Specialization in Project Management from EAESP-FGV. I am fluent in English and Spanish, and hold dual citizenship, Brazilian and Portuguese. My career aspiration is to continue driving company growth through data-driven insights and innovative strategies, exploring new technologies and analytical methodologies to solve complex challenges. I am always looking for opportunities to lead talented teams, develop creative solutions, and generate sustainable value for organizations. Knowledge: data analysis and science, Power BI, business intelligence, business plans, negotiation, strategy, strategic planning, segmentation, competitive analysis, and cost reduction.

The Kraft Heinz Company's Data Scientist compensation is $17,305 less than the US average for a Data Scientist. Data Scientist salaries at The Kraft Heinz Company in Chicago can range from $66,000 - $215,000.

results.title: Senior Staff Generative AI Scientist | Wfh
highlights:
Senior Staff Generative AI Scientist | Wfh
...
# Senior Staff Generative AI Scientist
...
2026
...
The Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI will apply knowledge and experience to real world problems and seek to utilize their skills reduce the cost of healthcare and improve health quality and outcomes. With access to dedicated on premise and cloud based big data solutions, the team can work with a vast amount of structured and unstructured data including claims, membership, physician demographics, medical records and others to begin to solve some of the most pressing healthcare issues of our time. A Data Scientist at Cotiviti will be given the opportunity to work directly with a team of healthcare professionals including analysts, clinicians, coding specialists, auditors and innovators to set aggressive goals and execute on them with the team. This is for an ambitious technologist, with the flexibility and personal drive to succeed in a dynamic environment where they are judged based on their direct impact to business outcomes.
...
- As a Sr Staff AI Scientist - Generative AI within Cotiviti you will be responsible for delivering solutions that help our clients identify payment integrity issues, reduce the cost of healthcare processes, or improve the quality of healthcare outcomes. You will work as part of a team and will be individually responsible for the delivery of value associated with your projects. You will be expected to follow processes and practices that allow your models to be incorporated into our machine learning platform for production execution and monitoring, however, initial exploratory data analysis allows for more flexible experimentation to discover solutions to the business problems presented.
...
- Work with key stakeholders within Research and Development as well as Operations, along with product management to assess the potential value and risks associated with business problems that have the potential to be solved using machine learning and Artificial Intelligence techniques.
...
- Develop an exploratory data analysis approach to verify the initial hypothesis associated with potential Artificial Intelligence/Machine Learning use cases.
...
- Document your approach, thinking and results in standard approaches to allow other data scientists to collaborate with you on this work.
...
- Prepare your final trained model and develop a validation test set for QA.
...
- Work with production operations to deploy your model into production and support them in monitoring model performance.
...
- Participate in other data science teams collaborating with your peers to support their projects.
...
- Participate in knowledge sharing sessions to bring new insights and technologies to the team.
...
- Participate in design sessions to continuously develop and improve the Cotiviti machine learning platform.
...
- Provide End to End value-based solutions, including data pipeline, model creation and application for end user consumption.
...
- PhD or Graduate degree in a quantitative discipline such as Computer Science/Engineering, Statistics, Operations Research covering Advanced Statistics, Machine learning and AI.
- Experience with the latest techniques in natural language processing including transformers, fine-tuning LLMs, measuring/benchmarking and deploying LLMs with tools such as HuggingFace, Langchain, LLAMA/Mistral and OpenAI, vector databases.
...
- 9+ years of hands-on data science/AI experience, using typical machine learning and data science tools including pandas, scikit-learn, keras, nltk, and TensorFlow/PyTorch, GPU.
...
- Experience building production-grade machine learning deployments on AWS, Azure, or GCP.
- Experience working with Apache Spark™ and large-scale distributed datasets.
...
- Experience communicating technical concepts to non-technical and technical audiences is a plus.
...
- Passion for collaboration, learn it all mindset and driving value with AI.
